action.reasoning: Observation: CLIP ViT-B/16 with 12 epochs only got 0.769 val bal_acc — much worse than ConvNeXt's 0.834. Analysis: CLIP backbone fine-tuning at LR=5e-5 may need more epochs, lower LR for backbone, or be undertrained. Looking at the trend, it was still improving at epoch 11. Also LR is plateauing too fast. ConvNeXt converged faster and to higher value. Decision: Stick with ConvNeXt-Tiny (best so far 0.8351). Try to push it further: train longer with better hyperparameters, possibly use larger image resolution variants. Since ConvNeXt at 20 epochs achieved 0.8351, try 30 epochs with stronger augmentation (cutmix), and slightly different schedule. Best.pt is currently the ConvNeXt — don't overwrite until improvement confirmed.
action.command: ARCH=convnext_tiny.fb_in22k_ft_in1k EPOCHS=30 LR=1e-4 LA_TAU=1.0 BS=64 python train.py
